description: Source-backed reference answers with known strengths, weaknesses, and evidence links for at least one release per selected lab. These are static reference snapshots as of July 2026; they serve as offline ground truth for evaluator calibration. Do not use as live benchmark data.
notes: All claims must be traceable to the linked source URL or a referenced evidence document.
Unknowns must be explicitly stated rather than omitted.
Safety card status must be one of: linked, not_found, not_applicable.
Benchmark status must be one of: supported (independently verified), vendor_provided (lab-reported only), contradicted, missing, not_comparable.
This file must not contain secrets, API keys, raw binary content, or raw crawled HTML.

whereItShines: Complex reasoning, scientific research, and long-context synthesis tasks
strengths: OpenAI reports GPT-5 achieves top results on MMLU and HumanEval benchmarks.
Available immediately via the OpenAI API and ChatGPT.
weaknesses: Vendor-reported benchmark claims have not been independently verified at oracle creation time.
Cost per token is higher than GPT-4o for comparable tasks.
knownUnknowns: Independent verification of vendor benchmark claims is not yet available.
Full parameter count has not been disclosed by OpenAI.

whereItShines: Agentic coding, tool use, browser/terminal workflows, and professional knowledge work
Cost-sensitive Sonnet-tier deployments that need performance near larger Opus-class models
strengths: Anthropic positions it as a substantial improvement over Sonnet 4.6 on reasoning, tool use, coding, and knowledge work.
Available across Claude plans, Claude Code, and the Claude API under the claude-sonnet-5 model name.
weaknesses: Anthropic reports higher misaligned-behavior rates than Opus 4.8 and Mythos Preview on its automated audit.
External benchmark corroboration is not yet independently verified.
knownUnknowns: Dangerous cyber capability specifics are only partially disclosed.

whereItShines: Complex reasoning, multimodal understanding, and long-document analysis
strengths: Google reports Gemini 2.5 Pro achieves top results on MMLU, GPQA, and coding benchmarks.
Native 1M-token context window with multimodal (image, video, audio, code) support.
weaknesses: Vendor-reported benchmark claims not independently verified at oracle creation time.
API pricing is higher than Gemini Flash tiers.
knownUnknowns: Independent verification of vendor benchmark claims is not yet available.

whereItShines: Open multimodal chat, coding, agentic tasks, and configurable reasoning in one smaller model family
Self-hosted or enterprise deployments needing Apache 2.0 licensing and lower serving cost
strengths: Mistral says Small 4 unifies Small, Magistral, Pixtral, and Devstral-style capabilities into one model.
256k context window, native text and image input, MoE architecture with 119B total parameters.
weaknesses: Vendor benchmark claims are not independently verified at oracle creation time.
A dedicated public safety or system card was not found by oracle metadata.

whereItShines: Coding, math, and reasoning at the frontier tier via DeepSeek-V4-Pro
strengths: DeepSeek reports V4-Pro achieves 92.1% on MMLU, 95.3% on HumanEval, 88.7% on MATH — vendor-provided results.
Open model weights available for research use.
API access available immediately for both V4-Pro and V4-Flash.
weaknesses: Vendor-reported benchmark claims have not been independently verified at oracle creation time.
HuggingFace links are evidence-only and must not be used as sendable release sources.
deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner will be deprecated on 2026-06-01.

evidenceLinks.note: evidence only, not a release source

knownUnknowns: Independent verification of all vendor benchmark claims pending.
Full training data details not disclosed.

whereItShines: Natively multimodal text-and-image tasks with 10M-token context
Open-source self-hosting under Llama 4 Community License
strengths: Scout (17B active params, 16 experts) and Maverick (17B active, 128 experts) are both natively multimodal.
Vendor reports 80.5% on MMLU and 88.7% on HumanEval for Scout — vendor-provided results.
weaknesses: Vendor-reported benchmark claims not independently verified at oracle creation time.
Llama 4 Community License includes restrictions not present in Apache 2.0.
knownUnknowns: Full training data details not disclosed.

whereItShines: Complex reasoning, scientific problem-solving, and GPQA-class tasks
strengths: xAI reports Grok 4 achieves 93.1% on GPQA Diamond and 96.7% on AIME 2024 — vendor-provided results.
256k token context window.
weaknesses: Vendor benchmark claims not independently verified at oracle creation time.
No dedicated public system card published with this release.
knownUnknowns: No public safety or system card available.

entries.title: NVIDIA Llama Nemotron Ultra open model delivers reasoning accuracy

whereItShines: Reasoning tasks including GPQA-class science and AIME-class math among open models
strengths: NVIDIA reports 84.0% on GPQA Diamond and 73.3% on AIME 2024 — vendor-provided results.
Open weights available on HuggingFace under an open license.
weaknesses: Vendor benchmark claims not independently verified at oracle creation time.
No dedicated public safety card for Nemotron Ultra.

entries.title: Introducing Nova-3: Deepgram's most accurate speech model

whereItShines: English speech-to-text accuracy at low latency for production applications
strengths: Deepgram reports Nova-3 achieves the lowest WER among its models on internal benchmarks.
weaknesses: Vendor-reported WER claims not independently verified at oracle creation time.
No dedicated public safety or system card.

entries.title: Eleven v3 is Now Generally Available

whereItShines: Production text-to-speech with improved handling of numbers, symbols, and specialized notation
strengths: ElevenLabs reports 72% user preference vs the alpha release in testing.
Internal benchmark error rate dropped from 15.3% to 4.9% across 27 categories.
weaknesses: Vendor-reported MOS and error-rate claims not independently verified at oracle creation time.
No dedicated public safety card.

whereItShines: Multilingual speech-to-text with high accuracy across diverse audio conditions
strengths: AssemblyAI reports Universal-1 achieves the lowest WER on their multilingual benchmarks — vendor-provided results.
weaknesses: Vendor-reported WER claims not independently verified at oracle creation time.
No dedicated public safety or system card.
